prompt: Where does it rain literally all the time?
system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: There is no place on Earth where it rains literally all the time. However, some locations experience very high annual rainfall and frequent rain. One of the wettest places on Earth is Mawsynram in Meghalaya, India, which receives an average annual rainfall of about 467.4 inches (11,871 millimeters). Another extremely wet location is Cherrapunji, also in Meghalaya, India. While these places receive a lot of rain, it does not rain continuously all the time.
